[01:11:02] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Remarketing Summary - Key Takeaways"
NÚMEROS: 20 ads exemplo, 20 ad sets, 3 segundos exclusion, 10 variations diárias (countdown ads)
VISUAL: Recap completo: remarketing happens by default → intentional setup structure → creative types → contextual scenarios → pocket audiences
[01:11:04]
"All right, cool. So, just to wrap it up, the big thing with remarketing is it already happens by default. You don't even have to intentionally set up a remarketing campaign."
[01:11:15]
"You can load up your original direct response campaign with remarketing assets if you want to. Just make sure they're not contextual remarketing assets."
[01:11:24]
"The contextual remarketing assets are like, 'Okay, if somebody opted into the webinar, I don't want an ad in my fucking webinar campaign that tells people like, Hey, you just went and watched my webinar. Go watch the replay.' That doesn't make any sense."
[01:11:37]
"That's a contextual remarketing audience. If you want to have an extremely aggressive remarketing campaign, you would set it up like this."
[01:11:45]
"You'd have the campaign, however many total remarketing ads you'd have, you'd have that many ad sets. So let's say you have 20 ads that you want to run. You'd have 20 individual ad sets targeting the same exact thing, whoever you want to reach with that remarketing campaign."
[01:12:01]
"Your exclusions would be the people who already converted. And in each ad set, you would exclude the 3-second video viewer of whatever ad is within that ad set."
[01:12:12]
"You'd have one ad per ad set. Each ad would be unique to that ad set. You publish the campaign first."
[01:12:20]
"That allows you to then go to the audiences section to see the specific video that you just published in the ads manager, create the 3-second video view audience, and then go set up the exclusions within that campaign that you just published."
[01:12:34]
"This is an awesome remarketing audience if you're going to set it up because it will rapidly cycle through a bunch of individual creatives assuming that the person watches to the 3-second threshold to then get excluded by it."
[01:12:47]
"This is the hammer them setup just with direct response ads. Hammer them and direct response, they go hand in hand. Hammer them is content, but you can set up a hammer them campaign with direct response ads instead of content."
[01:13:01]
"Same exact setup, just with direct response outcomes instead of content consumption."
[01:13:07]
"Remember, in terms of the specific types of direct response remarketing ads that are best, objection handlers, features, benefits, product, service outcomes, testimonials, expectations, just standard classic direct response can work well, too."
[01:13:23]
"Not even anything that acknowledges that they're in the remarketing process. Just cycling through a bunch of your already existing direct response ads and getting some serious use out of them. You can load it up that way, too."
[01:13:35]
"You can get a little more advanced. You could do team intros, demonstrate your facility and like how high quality your business is and your standards."
[01:13:44]
"You could obviously do character development, like the backstory on why you do what you do. The why content is really helpful."
[01:13:52]
"Keep in mind, all remarketing is is just essentially a sales conversation without the back and forth. So, you've got to successfully guess what's missing that remained to keep the person unconverted."
[01:14:04]
"Obviously, there'll never be an instance where we convert 100% of people. That's just not going to happen, you know?"
[01:14:11]
"So just like sales in real life, you know, we only close at whatever close rate we have because the other percentage of people, it just didn't make sense to do the deal, give or take the circumstances."
[01:14:23]
"You can also do when I say like context-based remarketing, I gave the example of a challenge funnel where we have an early bird window that ends."
[01:14:32]
"We then have an extended early bird window that ends and then we have the actual like challenge start date."
[01:14:39]
"During those days where we're getting closer to those things ending, we're going to do image ads that can quickly articulate some scarcity based urgency. Hey, this ends on this day. This ends in this many days."
[01:14:52]
"Those kind of like countdown based ads. And we're going to do essentially daily campaigns that change in that example."
[01:15:00]
"In addition to that, really important you understand this. There's so many different examples for like what we call pocket audience remarketing campaigns, which are just thinking with if this, then that scenarios."
[01:15:14]
"Like Tom did. If I'm pitching people through email and video pitches, then I'm going to remarket them with content, direct response ads to like go watch a webinar replay or to go book a call through ads, too. Why not?"
[01:15:28]
"Or like Daniel's example, like, okay, so if I have somebody that just opted into a webinar, why not pitch them on going and buying something or going and watching a remarketing replay?"
[01:15:39]
"Or in this example as well having a discount for not buying after a certain duration of time. Those campaigns, they do really well, too."
[01:15:48]
"The moral of the story is these are typically very high ROAS campaigns, but they're very low spend. So, they're advantageous if you stack them. They're advantageous if you stack them."
[01:16:00]
"That way, it becomes like a sizable enough amount of revenue to justify the effort of these types of campaigns."
[01:16:08]
"Typically by themselves, unless the audience sizes are large enough, it's not that easy to justify the effort relative to the fact that it's going to be a very low spend campaign with technically a very high rise, but a very low overall amount of dollars collected."
[01:16:24]
"So, I'm a big fan of pocket audience stacking. I want to think of a handful of pocket audiences and I want to exploit them."
[01:16:32]
"They're very great. It's a great method of the chunk method to be clear. Like rather than having one funnel that contributes all the revenue to the growth, you have that plus, you know, in this case a handful of pocket audiences that you're exploiting in real time to chunk some additional revenue on top of whatever you're doing."
[01:16:51]
"Now, big fan of remarketing with content, of course, I can't stress enough. I just tried to stay away from, you know, content remarketing on this call given I talk about it extensively all the time."
[01:17:02]
"I tried to center this call more so around direct response remarketing, but I do just want to pepper in. I am a huge fan, huge advocate. I pioneered these strategies."
[01:17:13]
"Content remarketing strategies is highly effective for putting content in front of people that can also help influence the sales process, but a little more indirectly since they don't have buttons to click through and convert on something."
[01:17:26]
"So, peppering in a lot of what you guys have learned from me from the content campaigns alongside of direct response remarketing campaigns. Again, a lot of effectiveness there."
[01:17:37]
"Okay, cool. Anything else? Anybody got anything else? Follow-up questions."
[01:17:44]
"Yep. Hey, Jeremy. Appreciate the presentation here. When we talk about remarketing, usually we send people to, you know, the VSSL funnel for it."
[01:17:54]
"I set up a remarketing campaign the other day, sending people to just a website with case studies and then it's click a button to work with us and there's like 30 YouTube videos on there and whatnot."
[01:18:05]
"Nice. When we're doing some of the remarketing, do you like to send them back to a similar page or do you like to put them through like a circuitous kind of route to other like other ways to expose them?"
[01:18:17]
"Say this is for someone who video views saw direct response ads but hasn't booked a call yet."
[01:18:24]
"Are you asking specifically whether that page example that you're using is the best thing to do there or what specifically are you asking?"
[01:18:33]
"Yeah, with the page example, what do you what do you..."
[01:18:37]
"And you could do a few. You could find out, Charles. It'd be a simple split test. You drive traffic back to the VSSL page that you already created."
[01:18:46]
"Drive traffic to another VSSL page with a different headline or a different VSSL on it to see if it gets it to convert."
[01:18:54]
"And in this example, your context is they went to the page and they didn't convert. Right."
[01:19:00]
"This is basically everyone who's seen a direct response ad whether they went to the page or not. I have like testimonial remarketing videos I have running."
[01:19:10]
"Understood. So, in that example, it make total sense since you're not just doing the web audience to still drive traffic back to the original direct response page, which is still happening within the original campaign you've already set up."
[01:19:24]
"Just to be clear, but you could take it a step further to your point, do a completely different funnel page that you're driving the traffic to to see if that works better."
[01:19:34]
"I would do a handful of pages in that example. It make no sense not to. It give you an opportunity."
[01:19:41]
"As I mentioned, remember, all remarketing is is just sales conversations without the back and forth. So, if somebody's unconverted because they want to see a fuckload of case studies, the way you're doing it now would have effectiveness."
[01:19:55]
"If somebody's unconverted because they saw an ad and they just never fucking clicked, it'd make a lot of sense to just drive them back to the original page."
[01:20:03]
"If somebody already clicked to the page and they didn't convert and maybe they're also not going to convert on your case study page, it makes sense to just throw another VSSL in front of them that has different talking points, you know, different frame."
[01:20:18]
"That's all remarketing is. It's just an opportunity to try to overcome these things that we don't know are holding people back. We're just kind of guessing. Does that make sense?"
[01:20:28]
"That does. I will test the different remarketing entry points. Appreciate it."
[01:20:33]
"No problem."
[01:20:35]
"Okay, cool. Brendan has a question. He says, 'Where are these recordings saved?'"
[01:20:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "How to Access Recordings"
NÚMEROS: 2019-presente (histórico completo disponível)
VISUAL: Tutorial navigation: Inner Circle library → Weekly Group Call Library page → Telegram chat (click name → search "group call recording" OR click Videos tab) → web portal (Jeremy AI with voice/video/memory)
[01:20:42]
"Sure, Brendan. Let's go through that real quick. So, first of all, when you join into the inner circle, you get access to a big library. And this library comes with all kinds of content."
[01:20:54]
"To be clear, I'm an admin, so I have literally all the content. You might not see nearly as many pages, just to be clear."
[01:21:02]

[01:21:11]

[01:21:21]
"This is also where you have the web portal version of Jeremy AI which gives you the ability to do like voice video and memory based chat."
[01:21:30]
"But to be clear, as soon as these get done, we upload them straight into here. And so you'll see as an example, in this case, this is last week's weekly group call since we're in the middle of filming this one right now."
[01:21:43]
"You got links to everything that we discuss inside of these. In addition to that, we upload the raw recording right inside of the Telegram group as well."
[01:21:53]

[01:22:07]
"As an example, including the one from last week, you also have the function to click the name at the top, whether it's on mobile or on desktop like you see me here now."
[01:22:17]

[01:22:25]

[01:22:43]
"And therefore every weekly group call is also in there as well. So, you have many places that you can access the information."

[01:22:59]
"We also just give you the ability to consume it right there in Telegram afterwards, so you don't have to go inside of the course library."

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
15 mínimo de criativos por ad set (Meta Andromeda)
25-30 criativos típicos por ad set
180 dias máximo para website audiences
3 segundos threshold para video view exclusions
100 mínimo de pessoas matched para remarketing audiences
150-200 pessoas recomendadas para upload
$47 early bird pricing (exemplo Challenge Funnel)
$97 VIP early bird
$77 extended early bird
$149 VIP extended
$30K+ daily spend (exemplo high-scale)
10 variations diárias de countdown ads
20,000 webinar registrants (exemplo large pocket audience)
10-20x ROAS típico em pocket audiences
$2-3K spend para run laps em pocket audiences
3-4x ROAS (exemplo DM ads Nick)
55% → 68% show rate improvement (Joseph case study)
50% → 59% cash collected improvement
40% close rate mantido
40% PIFFs average
$350K cash collected (Joseph scaled result)
3.5-4x ROAS (Joseph final)
$1.5M+ revenue Julho (Tom hyperbaric chambers)
2% lift negligível (19 confirmation page videos)
+14% lift adicional (urgency video)
$5,000 cirurgia cachorro (why content example)
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Remarketing Automático (Default)
Retargeting happens by default em todas campanhas
Frequency climb indica remarketing ativo
Plataformas servem ads para pessoas "probable to convert"
Não necessário setup manual de remarketing campaign
Meta Andromeda Requirements
Creative diversification obrigatória
Volume substancial de criativos necessário
Não desligar underperforming creatives (são retargeting assets)
Plataforma encoraja upload máximo de criativos
Account Limitations - Advantage Audiences
Processo 3-4 steps para true remarketing
Custom audience = suggestion se Advantage+ toggle ON
Necessário uncheck box no bottom left
Botão "control audience targeting" ou "further limit reach"
OG Remarketing Campaign Setup
1 ad por ad set (como Hammer Them)
3-second exclusion em cada video
Cycle through multiple direct response creators
Publish primeiro, depois criar exclusions
Audience Types para Remarketing
Unconverted web traffic (180 days max)
Exclusão: converted web traffic
Video viewers (ad videos)
Video viewers (organic content)
Specific video thresholds
Page messagers (DM ads)
Post savers (high intent)
Social interactions (FB/IG/TikTok/YouTube)
File Naming Pro Tip
Nome do arquivo = nome no audiences section
Usar schema: Ad 1, Ad 2, Ad 3, etc.
Facilita criação de 3sec exclusions
Correspondência direta ad set/ad/exclusion
Remarketing = Sales Without Back & Forth
Guessing game do que segura conversão
Não há feedback em tempo real
Necessário cycle múltiplos criativos
Inefficient comparado a sales calls
Creative Types Principais
Objection Handlers
Features
Product/Service Outcomes
Testimonials
Expectations
Classic Direct Response
Advanced Creative Types
Team Intros (upsell da equipe)
Facility Tours (demonstrar qualidade)
Character Development (backstory)
Why Content (múltiplas razões)
Backstory narratives
Countdown-Based Remarketing
Image ads diários
Scarcity real (early bird windows)
Daily campaigns com countdown
Multiple audience layers (web + cold)
Genuine scarcity conditioning
If This Then That Scenarios
Contextual remarketing situations
Webinar: opted/didn't show/showed/didn't convert
Different remarketing paths por scenario
Requires contextual landing pages
Pocket Audiences Strategy
High ROAS but low spend
Stacking múltiplos pockets = worth it
Large audiences justify effort
Small audiences = low hundreds spend
Backend Selling Systems (4 componentes)
Confirmation Page Best Practices
Hammer Them Campaign
Value Dense Emails
Setter/Closer Best Practices
Selfish Benchmark Test
"Would YOU talk to you?"
Self-assessment honesto
Low tolerance threshold awareness
Snap judgment reality
Thinking Like a Doctor Framework
List possible causes
Analyze symptoms/data
Prioritize by revenue impact
Efficiency consideration
Highest probability action first
ESTRUTURA:
Campaign Structure:
Remarketing Campaign
├── Ad Set 1 (unconverted web 180d)
│   ├── Include: unconverted web traffic
│   ├── Exclude: converted web traffic
│   ├── Exclude: 3sec video viewer Ad 1
│   └── Ad 1 (unique direct response)
├── Ad Set 2
│   ├── Include: unconverted web traffic
│   ├── Exclude: converted web traffic
│   ├── Exclude: 3sec video viewer Ad 2
│   └── Ad 2 (unique direct response)
└── Ad Set 3-10 (repeat structure)
When Intentional Remarketing Makes Sense:
Product/solution-aware audiences
Remain unconverted com main DR ads
Different creative set específico
Sizable enough audience (100+ matched)
Platform Philosophy:
Facebook/Google target small businesses
Automatic remarketing solution
Simplification for non-savvy users
Play into default = negate manual setup
Content vs Direct Response:
Content: indirect influence, no buttons
Direct Response: conversion-focused, CTAs
Both trabalham juntos
Remarketing with content = highly effective
Pocket Audience Stacking Math:
Pocket 1: $50K extra
Pocket 2: $100K extra
Pocket 3: $50K extra
Total: $200K (20% towards extra $1M/month)
Webinar Remarketing Decision Tree:
Opted In
├── Showed Up
│   ├── Booked → Hammer Them
│   └── Didn't Book → Discount/Replay/Objection Handling
└── Didn't Show
   └── Replay Remarketing
DM Ads Problem Diagnosis:
Ad/DM incongruency
Setters parecem bots/idiots
Mensagens sem appeal
Jump direto para pitch
Low IQ communication
Response dentro de 1 minuto (remarketing ineffective)
Case Studies:
Cody Sanchez: Goldman Sachs → Wall Street bias → Main Street Millionaire mission
Tom Hyperbaric: $1.5M July, Messi client, why content strategy
Joseph Backend Systems: 55%→68% show, 50%→59% cash, $350K scaled
Jeremy Confirmation Page: 19 videos = 2% lift, urgency video = +14% lift
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Typical Pocket Audience Spend: Low thousands/month
Small Pocket Audiences: Low hundreds/month
Large Pocket Audiences (20K+ people): $2-3K spend, tens of thousands revenue, potentially $100K+
High-Scale Daily Spend: $30K+ (countdown remarketing example)
ROI Pocket Audiences: 10-20x ROAS typical
Challenge Funnel Pricing Tiers:
Early Bird: $47 regular, $97 VIP
Extended Early Bird: $77 regular, $149 VIP
DM Ads Performance (Nick): 3-4x ROAS, declining weekly
Backend Systems Impact (Joseph):
Show Rate: 55% → 68% (+13%)
Cash Collected: 50% → 59% (+9%)
Close Rate: 40% maintained
PIFFs: 40% average
Scaled to: $350K cash collected
ROAS: 3.5-4x
Hyperbaric Chambers (Tom): $1.5M revenue (July)
Confirmation Page Testing:
19 videos: +2% lift (negligible)
Urgency video: +14% additional lift
DURAÇÃO: 1:23:30 FORMATO: Weekly Group Call / Masterclass
